Patch Set 2:

(2 comments)

Patch Set 2:

Do you know this issue, when trying to look at logs in the container,  the default vi editor, tail, etc .. don't wrap lines - LINES and COLUMNS are set up correctly?   Do you know how to get pass that, as this is important for debugging at this stage.

Patch Set 2:

One other comment since nss was added to explicitly deal with the omission in the previous version of the openjdk8 and now has been put back by the project, it could be removed from one of the base images.

Patch Set 2:

(2 comments)

> Do you know this issue, when trying to look at logs in the
 > container,  the default vi editor, tail, etc .. don't wrap lines -
 > LINES and COLUMNS are set up correctly?   Do you know how to get
 > pass that, as this is important for debugging at this stage.

Please look at https://en.wikibooks.org/wiki/Learning_the_vi_Editor/BusyBox_vi
I'm afraid that the only way is to install vim or other 'real' editor because alpine's vi is not full-featured vi (the same is true for tail and others) but busybox's clone. Unfortunately by adding vim we add 45Mb to image size.

Patch Set 2:

> One other comment since nss was added to explicitly deal with the
 > omission in the previous version of the openjdk8 and now has been
 > put back by the project, it could be removed from one of the base
 > images.

I've found it out only recently, so, yes, going to fix it.

Patch Set 2:

Thanks for the info about vim, I suspected that was the case, but thought the package would be smaller, we can table it for now.

I am not sure about the "apk upgrade" if it upgrades the OS packages, and you fix the image build against the 1.4.0 that in theory should be blessed, it pretty much defeats the purpose of having a blessed release image to avoid the recent problems we had with openjdk mutability.  I question is if a recommended practice too.

I think all the OS stuff should go to blessed parent images though to fix them in the same version and avoid mutability.   Only application stuff should be left here.  But we don't need to do that right now, may be we can walk towards that in the future.

nss removal can wait too.
Uploaded patch set 3.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

Need a resolution on the "upgrade" approach if it can be investigated best practice when we are inheriting from a fixed blessed image before letting this one proceed. Intuitively, to me doesn't seem right but I'd like to hear feedback and best practices on this.   I usually haven't come across upgrade operations in packaging in Dockerfiles.

Patch Set 3:

> Need a resolution on the "upgrade" approach if it can be
 > investigated best practice when we are inheriting from a fixed
 > blessed image before letting this one proceed. Intuitively, to me
 > doesn't seem right but I'd like to hear feedback and best practices
 > on this.   I usually haven't come across upgrade operations in
 > packaging in Dockerfiles.

You're absolutely right: https://docs.docker.com/v17.09/engine/userguide/eng-image/dockerfile_best-practices/#run

I'm not sure where from came this upgrade. So, fixing. Thank you for thoroughness in reviewing.
